provenance.disclosure_note: Official SEC XBRL companyfacts feed normalized into canonical financial statements.
Official SEC filing archive used for filing metadata, ownership, governance, and event disclosures.
Persisted chart-ready historical and forecast dashboard payloads derived from official company filings and labeled internal projection rules.
Cached model outputs derived from official filings, Treasury/Fed rates, and labeled price fallbacks.

summary.headline: Growth Outlook

secondary_badges.label: Quality
secondary_badges.detail: Margins and cash conversion from reported periods.

secondary_badges.label: Momentum
secondary_badges.detail: Base next-year growth and latest earnings drift.

secondary_badges.label: Value
secondary_badges.unavailable_reason: Hidden until a trustworthy valuation input set is available.

secondary_badges.label: Forecast Stability
secondary_badges.detail: Forecast stability uses the Technology sector template and 8 annual periods; historical backtests show 41.5% composite weighted APE (very_wide) across 6 point-in-time walk-forward snapshots with REVENUE 50%, EBIT 20%, EPS 15%, and FCF 15%; guidance status management_guidance_applied; scenario dispersion 5.5% bull/bear spread. This is a conservative stability signal, not statistical confidence.

summary.thesis: ORACLE CORP reported 11% 3Y revenue CAGR; the driver-based base case implies 8% next-year growth, with bull / bear cases of 11% and 5%.
unavailable_notes: Forecast values are dashed or muted and never presented as reported results.
Forecast stability is calibrated from point-in-time revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF walk-forward errors plus explicit risk penalties, not a probability or confidence interval.
Value stays explicitly unavailable until a trustworthy valuation input set exists.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.subtitle: Reported history with driver-based base / bull / bear scenarios
highlights: Base next-year revenue 8.5% via top-down proxy decomposition + guidance.
Base next-year EBIT margin 31.6% from explicit cost buckets.
Bull / Bear next-year EPS $5.53 / $4.39 after explicit interest, tax, and dilution schedules.

eps.title: EPS
eps.subtitle: Diluted EPS with explicit SBC and buyback dilution logic

growth_summary.title: Growth Summary
growth_summary.subtitle: Scenario outputs are shown without benchmark overlays until a trustworthy comparable series is available.

items.label: Revenue Method
items.detail: The default path decomposes realized growth into a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and a share / mix proxy; when segment history is available, the engine upgrades to a bottom-up segment rollup using the same proxy stack.

items.label: Price Proxy x Growth Stack
items.detail: No direct unit-volume dataset is available here; the combined volume proxy is the residual demand component plus the share / mix proxy after removing the pricing proxy from realized growth.

items.label: Residual Demand / Share-Mix Proxy
items.detail: No external market-size or true market-share dataset is wired into this engine, so residual demand growth and share / mix shift are inferred from realized growth after removing the pricing proxy. The bottom-up path applies the same proxy decomposition inside disclosed segments.

items.label: Management Guidance
items.detail: When earnings releases include revenue guidance, year-one revenue is anchored toward the guided midpoint without using later releases.

items.label: Cost Schedule
items.detail: Operating leverage is driven by separate variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost buckets.

items.label: Operating Working Capital
items.detail: Deferred revenue 0 days; accrued operating liabilities 0 days. Excludes cash, short-term investments, short-term debt, current maturities, and other financing items. AR median from disclosure; Inventory median from disclosure; AP median from disclosure; Deferred revenue assumed 0 when undisclosed; Accrued operating liabilities assumed 0 when undisclosed

items.label: Fixed-Capital Reinvestment
items.detail: Sales-to-capital sizes positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment only; delta operating working capital is modeled separately in operating cash flow so the bridge does not double count it.

items.label: Capex / Depreciation
items.detail: Capex is the higher of maintenance capex and depreciation plus positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment.

items.label: Below-The-Line Bridge
items.detail: Pretax income explicitly bridges from EBIT through interest expense, interest income, other income or expense, and taxes instead of using a flat EBIT-to-net conversion.

items.label: Cash + Debt Support
items.detail: Cash basis: Disclosed cash balance. Debt basis: Disclosed debt balance. Interest basis: Disclosed interest rates. Other basis: Zero other income fallback. Tax basis: Default tax rate.

items.label: Dilution Bridge
items.value: Proxy fallback from historical share drift
items.detail: Fallback basis: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies.

items.label: History Depth
items.detail: The driver engine falls back to the older heuristic path when the historical statement set is too thin to support explicit schedules.

items.label: Revenue Formula
items.value: Prior revenue x (1 + residual-demand effect + share/mix proxy effect + price proxy effect + price-volume cross term), then apply the year-one guidance blend
items.detail: FY2026E effects: residual demand 6.4%, share/mix 0.0%, price proxy 2.0%, cross term 0.1%, raw growth 8.5%. Driver seed decomposes realized growth into price proxy 2.2%, residual-implied demand growth 7.8%, and share/mix proxy 0.0%. Guidance anchor $1018.00 visible but skipped because it fails the 0.5x-1.6x sanity gate. Final FY2026E growth 8.5% drives revenue to $62.27B.

items.label: Operating Income Formula
items.value: Revenue - variable costs - semi-variable costs - fixed costs
items.detail: Base variable cost ratio 47.6%; semi-variable cost ratio 9.9%.

items.label: Pretax Income Formula
items.value: EBIT - interest expense + interest income + other income or expense
items.detail: Base FY2026E: EBIT $19.70B, interest expense $2.02B, interest income $221.0M, other $0.00, pretax $17.90B.

items.label: Tax Formula
items.value: Pretax income x effective tax rate
items.detail: Base FY2026E taxes $3.76B on pretax income $17.90B at 21.0%.

items.label: Capex Formula
items.value: max(maintenance capex, D&A + max(delta revenue, 0) / sales-to-capital)
items.detail: Base FY2026E: maintenance capex is the higher of $4.95B and D&A $3.57B; positive-growth fixed capital uses 0.36x sales-to-capital. Delta operating working capital $3.85B flows through OCF, not capex.

items.label: Operating Cash Flow Formula
items.value: Net income + D&A + SBC - delta operating working capital
items.detail: Base FY2026E: net income $14.14B + D&A $3.57B + SBC $3.33B - delta operating WC $3.85B = OCF $17.20B.

items.label: Free Cash Flow Formula
items.detail: Base FY2026E: OCF $17.20B - capex $17.22B = FCF $-21.7M.

items.label: Diluted EPS Formula
items.value: Net income / diluted shares, with basic shares rolled by proxy net dilution and diluted shares topped up by a latent dilution overlay
items.detail: FY2026E proxy fallback: starting basic 2.87B + proxy net change -1.2M = ending basic 2.86B; latent dilution overlay 0 from 0.0% reaches 2.86B diluted shares. Proxy basis: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies. EPS = $14.14B / 2.86B = $4.94.

items.label: Bottom-Up Basis
items.detail: When segment, geography, or product disclosures exist, the engine aggregates the forecast bottom-up before applying company-level overlays.

items.label: Growth Sensitivity
items.detail: Bull and bear demand plus share assumptions frame the next-year top-line sensitivity band.

items.label: Margin Sensitivity
items.detail: Variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules create the operating leverage range across scenarios.

items.label: Dilution Sensitivity
items.detail: SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert assumptions drive share-count sensitivity into EPS.

revenue_outlook_bridge.title: Revenue Outlook Bridge
revenue_outlook_bridge.subtitle: Year-one bridge from the last reported period into the base forecast

points.annotation: Last reported revenue carried into the FY2026E bridge.

highlights: FY2025 reported revenue bridges into FY2026E base revenue through Residual Demand Effect, Share / Mix Effect, Price Proxy Effect, and Price-Volume Cross Term.
Forecast bridge values remain internal projections rather than reported results.

margin_path.title: Margin Path
margin_path.subtitle: Gross, operating, and net margin across reported and projected periods

points.annotation: Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.
Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.
Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.

highlights: Reported and projected margin paths remain separated so forecast ratios never read as filed results.

fcf_outlook.title: FCF Outlook
fcf_outlook.subtitle: Path from earnings and cash-flow inputs into free cash flow
highlights: Reported and forecast cash-flow bridge items remain labeled separately so the FCF path stays audit-friendly.
Base-case FCF bridge keeps net income, D&A, SBC, operating working capital, and capex explicit in forecast mode.

forecast_methodology.label: Driver-based integrated forecast
forecast_methodology.summary: Revenue is modeled from a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and share or mix proxies, then layered with segment rollups, guidance, and backlog or capacity overlays when available. EBIT flows from explicit variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules; operating working capital is forecast through receivables, inventory, payables, deferred revenue, and accrued operating-liability days while excluding cash and financing items; pretax income then bridges through debt-funded interest expense, cash yield, and other income or expense; operating cash flow subtracts delta operating working capital, capex covers maintenance capital plus positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment from sales-to-capital, and free cash flow and diluted EPS are layered on top with disclosed cash, debt, SBC, buybacks, acquisition dilution, and convert dilution where available. Forecast Stability is then calibrated against point-in-time walk-forward backtests for revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF before conservative penalties are applied. When disclosure is sparse, the engine uses conservative component-level fallbacks before dropping all the way back to the older guarded heuristic path.
forecast_methodology.disclaimer: Scenario outputs are internally derived from official inputs and remain explicitly labeled as forecast rather than reported results or analyst consensus.

forecast_diagnostics.summary: Forecast stability uses the Technology sector template and 8 annual periods; historical backtests show 41.5% composite weighted APE (very_wide) across 6 point-in-time walk-forward snapshots with REVENUE 50%, EBIT 20%, EPS 15%, and FCF 15%; guidance status management_guidance_applied; scenario dispersion 5.5% bull/bear spread. This is a conservative stability signal, not statistical confidence.

components.label: Historical backtests
components.detail: Point-in-time walk-forward revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF errors anchor the stability score before risk penalties are applied. Metric weights: REVENUE 50%, EBIT 20%, EPS 15%, and FCF 15%.

components.label: History depth
components.detail: Short public history limits the number of walk-forward snapshots available for calibration.

components.label: Cyclicality
components.detail: Large swings in historical revenue growth make future driver assumptions less stable.

components.label: Structural breaks
components.detail: Recent growth or margin regime shifts reduce trust in extrapolating earlier history.

components.label: Major M&A
components.detail: Acquisition-heavy periods can break comparability between historical and forecast economics.

components.label: Accounting restatements
components.detail: Recent restatements lower confidence that past reported drivers are a stable base for forecasting.

components.label: Share-count stability
components.detail: Unstable diluted share counts weaken EPS stability even when revenue forecasts are reasonable.

components.label: Scenario dispersion
components.detail: Wide spread between bull, base, and bear cases keeps the stability score conservative.

components.label: Parser confidence
components.detail: Low or missing parser confidence is treated as a conservative penalty rather than a positive boost.

components.label: Missing-data penalty
components.detail: Missing revenue history or missing quality signals reduce the score rather than being silently imputed.

methodology.label: Driver-based integrated forecast
methodology.summary: Revenue is modeled from a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and share or mix proxies, then layered with segment rollups, guidance, and backlog or capacity overlays when available. EBIT flows from explicit variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules; operating working capital is forecast through receivables, inventory, payables, deferred revenue, and accrued operating-liability days while excluding cash and financing items; pretax income then bridges through debt-funded interest expense, cash yield, and other income or expense; operating cash flow subtracts delta operating working capital, capex covers maintenance capital plus positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment from sales-to-capital, and free cash flow and diluted EPS are layered on top with disclosed cash, debt, SBC, buybacks, acquisition dilution, and convert dilution where available. Forecast Stability is then calibrated against point-in-time walk-forward backtests for revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF before conservative penalties are applied. When disclosure is sparse, the engine uses conservative component-level fallbacks before dropping all the way back to the older guarded heuristic path.
methodology.disclaimer: Scenario outputs are internally derived from official inputs and remain explicitly labeled as forecast rather than reported results or analyst consensus.

2026.formula_template: Prior revenue x (1 + residual demand + share/mix proxy + price proxy + price-volume cross term)
2026.formula_computation: $57.40B x (1 + 8.5%) = $62.27B. Driver stack: residual demand 6.4%, share/mix 0.0%, price proxy 2.0%, cross term 0.1%. Guidance anchor $1018.00 visible but skipped because it fails the 0.5x-1.6x sanity gate.

items.detail: Fallback basis: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies.

items.label: History Depth
items.detail: The driver engine falls back to the older heuristic path when the historical statement set is too thin to support explicit schedules.

items.label: Revenue Formula
items.value: Prior revenue x (1 + residual-demand effect + share/mix proxy effect + price proxy effect + price-volume cross term), then apply the year-one guidance blend
items.detail: FY2026E effects: residual demand 6.4%, share/mix 0.0%, price proxy 2.0%, cross term 0.1%, raw growth 8.5%. Driver seed decomposes realized growth into price proxy 2.2%, residual-implied demand growth 7.8%, and share/mix proxy 0.0%. Guidance anchor $1018.00 visible but skipped because it fails the 0.5x-1.6x sanity gate. Final FY2026E growth 8.5% drives revenue to $62.27B.

items.label: Operating Income Formula
items.value: Revenue - variable costs - semi-variable costs - fixed costs
items.detail: Base variable cost ratio 47.6%; semi-variable cost ratio 9.9%.

items.label: Pretax Income Formula
items.value: EBIT - interest expense + interest income + other income or expense
items.detail: Base FY2026E: EBIT $19.70B, interest expense $2.02B, interest income $221.0M, other $0.00, pretax $17.90B.

items.label: Tax Formula
items.value: Pretax income x effective tax rate
items.detail: Base FY2026E taxes $3.76B on pretax income $17.90B at 21.0%.

items.label: Capex Formula
items.value: max(maintenance capex, D&A + max(delta revenue, 0) / sales-to-capital)
items.detail: Base FY2026E: maintenance capex is the higher of $4.95B and D&A $3.57B; positive-growth fixed capital uses 0.36x sales-to-capital. Delta operating working capital $3.85B flows through OCF, not capex.

items.label: Operating Cash Flow Formula
items.value: Net income + D&A + SBC - delta operating working capital
items.detail: Base FY2026E: net income $14.14B + D&A $3.57B + SBC $3.33B - delta operating WC $3.85B = OCF $17.20B.

items.label: Free Cash Flow Formula
items.detail: Base FY2026E: OCF $17.20B - capex $17.22B = FCF $-21.7M.

items.label: Diluted EPS Formula
items.value: Net income / diluted shares, with basic shares rolled by proxy net dilution and diluted shares topped up by a latent dilution overlay
items.detail: FY2026E proxy fallback: starting basic 2.87B + proxy net change -1.2M = ending basic 2.86B; latent dilution overlay 0 from 0.0% reaches 2.86B diluted shares. Proxy basis: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies. EPS = $14.14B / 2.86B = $4.94.

items.label: Bottom-Up Basis
items.detail: When segment, geography, or product disclosures exist, the engine aggregates the forecast bottom-up before applying company-level overlays.

items.label: Growth Sensitivity
items.detail: Bull and bear demand plus share assumptions frame the next-year top-line sensitivity band.

items.label: Margin Sensitivity
items.detail: Variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules create the operating leverage range across scenarios.

items.label: Dilution Sensitivity
items.detail: SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert assumptions drive share-count sensitivity into EPS.

revenue_outlook_bridge.title: Revenue Outlook Bridge
revenue_outlook_bridge.subtitle: Year-one bridge from the last reported period into the base forecast

points.annotation: Last reported revenue carried into the FY2026E bridge.

highlights: FY2025 reported revenue bridges into FY2026E base revenue through Residual Demand Effect, Share / Mix Effect, Price Proxy Effect, and Price-Volume Cross Term.
Forecast bridge values remain internal projections rather than reported results.

margin_path.title: Margin Path
margin_path.subtitle: Gross, operating, and net margin across reported and projected periods

points.annotation: Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.
Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.
Projected gross margin holds the driver cost-of-revenue ratio.

highlights: Reported and projected margin paths remain separated so forecast ratios never read as filed results.

fcf_outlook.title: FCF Outlook
fcf_outlook.subtitle: Path from earnings and cash-flow inputs into free cash flow
highlights: Reported and forecast cash-flow bridge items remain labeled separately so the FCF path stays audit-friendly.
Base-case FCF bridge keeps net income, D&A, SBC, operating working capital, and capex explicit in forecast mode.

methodology.label: Driver-based integrated forecast
methodology.summary: Revenue is modeled from a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and share or mix proxies, then layered with segment rollups, guidance, and backlog or capacity overlays when available. EBIT flows from explicit variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules; operating working capital is forecast through receivables, inventory, payables, deferred revenue, and accrued operating-liability days while excluding cash and financing items; pretax income then bridges through debt-funded interest expense, cash yield, and other income or expense; operating cash flow subtracts delta operating working capital, capex covers maintenance capital plus positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment from sales-to-capital, and free cash flow and diluted EPS are layered on top with disclosed cash, debt, SBC, buybacks, acquisition dilution, and convert dilution where available. Forecast Stability is then calibrated against point-in-time walk-forward backtests for revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF before conservative penalties are applied. When disclosure is sparse, the engine uses conservative component-level fallbacks before dropping all the way back to the older guarded heuristic path.
methodology.disclaimer: Scenario outputs are internally derived from official inputs and remain explicitly labeled as forecast rather than reported results or analyst consensus.

forecast_diagnostics.summary: Forecast stability uses the Technology sector template and 8 annual periods; historical backtests show 41.5% composite weighted APE (very_wide) across 6 point-in-time walk-forward snapshots with REVENUE 50%, EBIT 20%, EPS 15%, and FCF 15%; guidance status management_guidance_applied; scenario dispersion 5.5% bull/bear spread. This is a conservative stability signal, not statistical confidence.

components.label: Historical backtests
components.detail: Point-in-time walk-forward revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF errors anchor the stability score before risk penalties are applied. Metric weights: REVENUE 50%, EBIT 20%, EPS 15%, and FCF 15%.

components.label: History depth
components.detail: Short public history limits the number of walk-forward snapshots available for calibration.

components.label: Cyclicality
components.detail: Large swings in historical revenue growth make future driver assumptions less stable.

components.label: Structural breaks
components.detail: Recent growth or margin regime shifts reduce trust in extrapolating earlier history.

components.label: Major M&A
components.detail: Acquisition-heavy periods can break comparability between historical and forecast economics.

components.label: Accounting restatements
components.detail: Recent restatements lower confidence that past reported drivers are a stable base for forecasting.

components.label: Share-count stability
components.detail: Unstable diluted share counts weaken EPS stability even when revenue forecasts are reasonable.

components.label: Scenario dispersion
components.detail: Wide spread between bull, base, and bear cases keeps the stability score conservative.

components.label: Parser confidence
components.detail: Low or missing parser confidence is treated as a conservative penalty rather than a positive boost.

components.label: Missing-data penalty
components.detail: Missing revenue history or missing quality signals reduce the score rather than being silently imputed.

studio.title: Projection Studio
studio.summary: Inspection of projected values, sensitivities, waterfall bridges, and traceable formulas.

methodology.label: Driver-based integrated forecast
methodology.summary: Revenue is modeled from a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and share or mix proxies, then layered with segment rollups, guidance, and backlog or capacity overlays when available. EBIT flows from explicit variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost schedules; operating working capital is forecast through receivables, inventory, payables, deferred revenue, and accrued operating-liability days while excluding cash and financing items; pretax income then bridges through debt-funded interest expense, cash yield, and other income or expense; operating cash flow subtracts delta operating working capital, capex covers maintenance capital plus positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment from sales-to-capital, and free cash flow and diluted EPS are layered on top with disclosed cash, debt, SBC, buybacks, acquisition dilution, and convert dilution where available. Forecast Stability is then calibrated against point-in-time walk-forward backtests for revenue, EBIT, EPS, and FCF before conservative penalties are applied. When disclosure is sparse, the engine uses conservative component-level fallbacks before dropping all the way back to the older guarded heuristic path.
methodology.disclaimer: Scenario outputs are internally derived from official inputs and remain explicitly labeled as forecast rather than reported results or analyst consensus.

drivers_used.title: Revenue Drivers
drivers_used.detail: The default path decomposes realized growth into a pricing proxy, residual-implied demand growth, and a share / mix proxy; when segment history is available, the engine upgrades to a bottom-up segment rollup using the same proxy stack.

drivers_used.title: Cost Structure
drivers_used.detail: Operating leverage is driven by separate variable, semi-variable, and fixed cost buckets.

drivers_used.title: Operating Working Capital
drivers_used.detail: Deferred revenue 0 days; accrued operating liabilities 0 days. Excludes cash, short-term investments, short-term debt, current maturities, and other financing items. AR median from disclosure; Inventory median from disclosure; AP median from disclosure; Deferred revenue assumed 0 when undisclosed; Accrued operating liabilities assumed 0 when undisclosed
default_markers: Derived from SEC revenue and operating income inputs plus D&A trace confidence default

drivers_used.title: Reinvestment + Capex
drivers_used.detail: Sales-to-capital sizes positive-growth fixed-capital reinvestment only; delta operating working capital is modeled separately in operating cash flow so the bridge does not double count it.
default_markers: Default depreciation ratio from capex intensity
Derived from D&A trace confidence default
Derived from capex trace confidence default
fallback_markers: Derived from OCF trace confidence fallback

drivers_used.title: Below-The-Line Bridge
drivers_used.detail: Pretax income explicitly bridges from EBIT through interest expense, interest income, other income or expense, and taxes instead of using a flat EBIT-to-net conversion.
default_markers: Derived from income tax trace confidence default
fallback_markers: Derived from pretax income trace confidence fallback

drivers_used.title: Dilution Bridge
drivers_used.value: Proxy fallback from historical share drift
drivers_used.detail: Fallback basis: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies.
fallback_markers: Historical diluted-share growth with revenue-scaled SBC, buyback, acquisition, and convert proxies
